# kubernetes.recipes — Complete Index

> All 1056 Kubernetes recipes with descriptions.

## Ai (149 recipes)

- [Agentic AI and Multi-Agent Systems](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/agentic-ai-multiagent-kubernetes/): Deploy autonomous AI agents and multi-agent orchestration on Kubernetes. LangGraph, CrewAI, AutoGen, tool-calling agents, agent-to-agent communication.
- [Batch AI Workloads with Volcano Scheduler](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/ai-batch-processing-volcano/): Volcano gang scheduling on Kubernetes: batch AI training, fair-share queues, minAvailable, job plugins, and GPU preemption. Complete setup guide.
- [AI Content Watermarking on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/ai-content-watermarking-kubernetes/): Deploy AI-generated content watermarking on Kubernetes. Invisible watermarks, SynthID integration, detection APIs, image and text watermarking pipelines.
- [AI Infrastructure Cost Optimization](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/ai-infrastructure-cost-optimization-kubernetes/): Optimize AI infrastructure costs on Kubernetes. GPU sharing, spot instances, inference batching, model quantization, token economics.
- [AI-Native Development Platforms on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/ai-native-development-kubernetes/): Build AI-native development platforms on Kubernetes. AI coding agents, automated testing, Copilot infrastructure, dev containers, and AI-driven CI/CD pipelines.
- [AI Resource Allocation Optimization](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/ai-resource-allocation-optimization-kubernetes/): Optimize GPU and memory allocation for AI workloads on Kubernetes. Right-size GPU requests, bin-packing strategies, gang scheduling.
- [AI Supercomputing on Kubernetes GPU Clusters](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/ai-supercomputing-kubernetes-gpu-clusters/): Build AI supercomputing platforms on Kubernetes. Multi-node GPU training, NVIDIA DGX SuperPOD, InfiniBand RDMA, NCCL tuning, Blackwell clusters.
- [AIPerf Benchmark LLMs on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/aiperf-benchmark-llm-kubernetes/): Deploy NVIDIA AIPerf to benchmark LLM inference performance on Kubernetes. Measure TTFT, ITL, throughput with real-time dashboard and GPU telemetry.
- [AIPerf Concurrency Sweep on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/aiperf-concurrency-sweep-kubernetes/): Run AIPerf concurrency sweeps on Kubernetes to find optimal LLM serving capacity. Automate 1-128 concurrent user benchmarks with batch Jobs.
- [AIPerf Goodput and SLO Benchmarks](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/aiperf-goodput-slo-benchmark/): Measure LLM goodput with AIPerf on Kubernetes. Define SLOs for TTFT and ITL, calculate effective throughput, and benchmark with timeslice analysis.
- [AIPerf LLM Benchmarking on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/aiperf-llm-benchmarking-kubernetes/): Benchmark generative AI inference on Kubernetes with NVIDIA AIPerf. Measure TTFT, ITL, throughput, and latency across vLLM, NIM.
- [AIPerf Multi-Model Benchmark on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/aiperf-multi-model-benchmark/): Compare multiple LLM models and backends with AIPerf on Kubernetes. Benchmark vLLM vs TGI vs Triton with automated multi-run confidence intervals.
- [AIPerf Trace Replay Benchmarks on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/aiperf-trace-replay-kubernetes/): Replay production traffic traces with AIPerf on Kubernetes. Use moon_cake format, ShareGPT datasets, and fixed schedules for realistic LLM benchmarks.
- [AIPerf Offline vLLM Benchmarking](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/aiperf-vllm-benchmark-offline/): Benchmark vLLM inference with AIPerf in air-gapped Kubernetes clusters. Use dummy tokenizers, offline mode, custom endpoints.
- [Autonomous Industrial Systems on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/autonomous-industrial-systems-kubernetes/): Orchestrate autonomous factories and logistics with Kubernetes. Digital twins, robot fleet coordination, industrial IoT pipelines, predictive maintenance.
- [CNCF AI Projects Landscape Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/cnai-cncf-ai-projects-landscape/): Navigate the CNCF AI project landscape for Kubernetes. Kubeflow, KServe, KAITO, Volcano, and emerging projects for training, serving, scheduling.
- [Compare NCCL Intra-Node vs Inter-Node Perfo...](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/compare-nccl-intra-inter-node/): Build a repeatable comparison between local and cross-node NCCL throughput to validate GPU cluster interconnect scaling and identify bottlenecks early.
- [Copy NVIDIA NIM Images to Internal Quay Reg...](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/copy-nim-image-internal-quay-registry/): Pull NIM container images from nvcr.io and push to an internal Quay registry. Covers authentication, tagging, air-gapped workflows, and curl token issues.
- [Dell PowerEdge XE7740 GPU Node Setup](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/dell-poweredge-xe7740-gpu-setup/): Configure Dell PowerEdge XE7740 GPU nodes with H200 GPUs for OpenShift and Kubernetes including BIOS, power, cooling, and network setup.
- [Deploy Fish Audio TTS on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-fish-audio-tts-kubernetes/): Deploy Fish Audio S2-Pro 5B text-to-speech model on Kubernetes for high-quality voice synthesis with multi-speaker support and streaming audio.
- [Deploy GLM-5 754B on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-glm5-754b-kubernetes/): Deploy Zhipu AI GLM-5 754B model on Kubernetes with vLLM. One of the largest open-weight models with multi-node tensor parallelism across 8+ GPUs.
- [Deploy Granite 4.0 Speech on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-granite-speech-kubernetes/): Deploy IBM Granite 4.0 1B Speech model on Kubernetes for automatic speech recognition. Lightweight 2B model runs on CPU or small GPU for STT workloads.
- [Deploy Kimi K2.5 1.1T MoE on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-kimi-k25-kubernetes/): Deploy Moonshot AI Kimi-K2.5 1.1T MoE multimodal model on Kubernetes. The largest open MoE model with 2.69M downloads for frontier AI tasks.
- [Deploy Llama 2 70B on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-llama2-70b-kubernetes/): Deploy Meta Llama 2 70B on Kubernetes with multi-GPU tensor parallelism, vLLM serving, and production-ready health checks and resource limits.
- [Deploy Llama 3.1 8B Instruct on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-llama31-8b-kubernetes/): Deploy Meta Llama 3.1 8B Instruct on Kubernetes with vLLM. Production-ready single-GPU deployment with 128K context, tool calling, and autoscaling.
- [Deploy LTX Video Generation on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-ltx-video-kubernetes/): Deploy Lightricks LTX-2.3 image-to-video model on Kubernetes for AI video generation with batch processing and S3 output storage.
- [Deploy MiniMax M2.5 229B on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-minimax-m25-kubernetes/): Deploy MiniMax M2.5 229B model on Kubernetes with vLLM. High-performance LLM with 485K downloads, optimized for multi-turn conversation and long context.
- [Deploy Mistral 7B with NVIDIA NIM](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-mistral-nvidia-nim/): Step-by-step guide to deploy Mistral-7B using NVIDIA NIM with TensorRT-LLM backend on Kubernetes for optimized GPU inference.
- [Deploy Mistral 7B with vLLM on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-mistral-vllm-kubernetes/): Step-by-step guide to deploy Mistral-7B-v0.1 using vLLM as an OpenAI-compatible inference server on Kubernetes with GPU fractioning.
- [Deploy Multinode NIM Models on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-multinode-nim-models-kubernetes/): Run large language models across multiple GPU nodes with NVIDIA NIM. Tensor parallelism, NCCL, InfiniBand, and Kubernetes Job orchestration.
- [Deploy NVIDIA Nemotron 120B MoE on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-nemotron-120b-kubernetes/): Deploy NVIDIA Nemotron-3-Super-120B-A12B MoE model on Kubernetes. 120B total parameters with 12B active for enterprise-grade inference.
- [Deploy Microsoft Phi-4 on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-phi4-kubernetes/): Deploy Microsoft Phi-4 small language model on Kubernetes with vLLM. Efficient 14B model with GPT-4 level reasoning on a single GPU.
- [Deploy Phi-4 Reasoning Vision on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-phi4-reasoning-vision-kubernetes/): Deploy Microsoft Phi-4-reasoning-vision-15B on Kubernetes for multimodal chain-of-thought reasoning with visual understanding on a single GPU.
- [Deploy Qwen3 235B MoE on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-qwen3-235b-moe-kubernetes/): Deploy Alibaba Qwen3-235B-A22B mixture-of-experts model on Kubernetes. Only 22B parameters active per token for efficient 235B-class inference.
- [Deploy Qwen3 Coder 80B on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-qwen3-coder-kubernetes/): Deploy Qwen3-Coder-Next 80B on Kubernetes for code generation, review, and refactoring. Production-ready AI coding assistant with multi-GPU serving.
- [Deploy Qwen3 TTS on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-qwen3-tts-kubernetes/): Deploy Qwen3-TTS-12Hz-1.7B-CustomVoice on Kubernetes for text-to-speech with custom voice cloning. 1.13M downloads, lightweight single-GPU deployment.
- [Deploy Qwen3.5 35B MoE on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-qwen35-35b-moe-kubernetes/): Deploy Alibaba Qwen3.5-35B-A3B mixture-of-experts multimodal model on Kubernetes. 35B total parameters with only 3B active for ultra-efficient inference.
- [Deploy Qwen3.5 397B MoE on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-qwen35-397b-moe-kubernetes/): Deploy Alibaba Qwen3.5-397B-A17B MoE multimodal model on Kubernetes. 397B total parameters with only 17B active per token for frontier VLM inference.

- [RetinaNet Object Detection on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-retinanet-kubernetes/): Deploy RetinaNet object detection model on Kubernetes with Triton Inference Server, TensorRT optimization, and batch processing pipelines.
- [Deploy Sarvam 105B on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-sarvam-105b-kubernetes/): Deploy Sarvam 105B multilingual LLM on Kubernetes with vLLM. India's largest open language model with native support for 10+ Indic languages.
- [Stable Diffusion XL on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-stable-diffusion-xl-kubernetes/): Deploy Stable Diffusion XL for image generation on Kubernetes with TensorRT acceleration, queued batch processing, and S3 output storage.
- [Deploy Whisper Speech-to-Text on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/deploy-whisper-kubernetes/): Deploy OpenAI Whisper for speech-to-text on Kubernetes with faster-whisper, batch transcription Jobs, and real-time streaming endpoints.
- [DGX H100 GPU Topology nvidia-smi](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/dgx-h100-gpu-topology-nvidia-smi/): Inspect DGX H100 GPU topology with nvidia-smi topo -m. NVSwitch NV18 links, cross-socket detection, PCIe hierarchy, and NCCL performance validation.
- [Distributed Inference Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/distributed-inference-kubernetes/): Deploy distributed LLM inference with tensor parallelism across multiple GPUs and pipeline parallelism across nodes on Kubernetes.
- [Distributed Inference with Run:ai](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/distributed-inference-runai-kubernetes/): Deploy distributed AI inference with NVIDIA Run:ai on Kubernetes. Single-node Knative, multinode LeaderWorkerSet, NIM, autoscaling, and observability.
- [Distributed Training TensorFlow PyTorch](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/distributed-training-tensorflow-pytorch-kubernetes/): Run distributed training jobs on Kubernetes with TensorFlow and PyTorch. Training Operator, multi-worker strategies, NCCL configuration.
- [DOCA Perftest RDMA Benchmarking](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/doca-perftest-rdma-benchmark-kubernetes/): Run NVIDIA DOCA perftest on Kubernetes to benchmark RDMA bandwidth and latency between GPU nodes. Traffic patterns, GPUDirect memory modes.
- [Domain-Specific Language Models on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/domain-specific-language-models-kubernetes/): Deploy and fine-tune domain-specific LLMs on Kubernetes. Legal, healthcare, finance, and code models with LoRA fine-tuning, NIM serving, and RAG pipelines.
- [Feast Feature Store Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/feast-feature-store-kubernetes/): Deploy Feast feature store on Kubernetes for ML feature management. Offline and online stores, feature serving, point-in-time joins.
- [GenAI-Perf Profile LLM Benchmark](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/genai-perf-benchmark-kubernetes/): Benchmark LLM inference with GenAI-Perf on Kubernetes. TTFT, throughput profiling, --service-kind openai, and vLLM vs TRT-LLM performance comparison.
- [GenAI-Perf Benchmark LLM Serving](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/genai-perf-benchmark-llm/): Benchmark LLM inference endpoints with NVIDIA GenAI-Perf for throughput, latency percentiles, time-to-first-token, and ITL metrics.
- [GenAI-Perf Benchmark Triton on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/genai-perf-triton-benchmark/): Benchmark NVIDIA Triton Inference Server performance on Kubernetes using GenAI-Perf. Measure TTFT, inter-token latency, throughput, and GPU telemetry.
- [GitOps for AI Workloads on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/gitops-ai-workloads-kubernetes/): Deploy AI models with GitOps on Kubernetes. Version ML models in Git, ArgoCD for model rollouts, Flux for GPU cluster sync.
- [Enable GPUDirect Storage in ClusterPolicy](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/gpu-clusterpolicy-gds-enable/): Enable NVIDIA GPUDirect Storage (GDS) in the GPU Operator ClusterPolicy for direct GPU-to-NVMe data paths. Driver module configuration and verification.
- [GPU Sharing MIG and Time-Slicing Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/gpu-sharing-mig-timeslicing-kubernetes/): Share GPUs across multiple pods with NVIDIA MIG and time-slicing on Kubernetes. MIG profiles for A100/H100, time-slicing configuration.
- [GPU Time-Slicing on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/gpu-time-slicing-kubernetes/): Share GPUs across multiple workloads using NVIDIA time-slicing on Kubernetes. Configure the device plugin, set replica counts, and manage fairness.
- [Inter-Node Tensor Parallelism on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/inter-node-tensor-parallelism-kubernetes/): Split a single LLM across multiple physical servers using tensor parallelism. Configure vLLM, NIM, and Ray for inter-node TP with NCCL over RDMA or TCP.
- [GPU Sharing and Bin Packing with KAI Scheduler](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kai-scheduler-gpu-sharing/): Maximize GPU utilization with KAI Scheduler GPU sharing, fractional GPUs, and bin packing strategies for Kubernetes AI workloads.
- [Installing NVIDIA KAI Scheduler AI Workloads](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kai-scheduler-installation/): Deploy KAI Scheduler for optimized GPU resource allocation in Kubernetes AI/ML clusters with hierarchical queues and batch scheduling
- [Batch Scheduling PodGroups in KAI Scheduler](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kai-scheduler-podgroups/): Implement gang scheduling for distributed training jobs using KAI Scheduler PodGroups to ensure all-or-nothing pod scheduling
- [Hierarchical Queues & Resource Fairness KAI...](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kai-scheduler-queues/): Configure hierarchical queues in KAI Scheduler for multi-tenant GPU clusters with quotas, limits, and Dominant Resource Fairness (DRF)
- [Topology-Aware Scheduling with KAI Scheduler](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kai-scheduler-topology-aware/): Optimize GPU workload placement using KAI Scheduler's Topology-Aware Scheduling (TAS) for NVLink, NVSwitch, and disaggregated serving architectures
- [KAITO AI Model Inference Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kaito-ai-inference-kubernetes/): Deploy AI models with KAITO (Kubernetes AI Toolchain Operator) for automated GPU provisioning and model serving.
- [Katib Hyperparameter Tuning Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/katib-hyperparameter-tuning-kubernetes/): Automate hyperparameter tuning with Katib on Kubernetes. Bayesian optimization, random search, grid search, early stopping.
- [KnativeServing for AI Inference OpenShift](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/knative-serving-nim-inference-openshift/): Configure KnativeServing with scale-to-zero, GPU scheduling features, Kourier ingress, and custom domain templates for AI inference workloads on OpenShift.
- [KServe Model Serving Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kserve-model-serving-kubernetes/): Deploy ML models with KServe for serverless inference on Kubernetes. InferenceService, scale-to-zero, canary rollouts, model transformers.
- [Distrib. Training Kubeflow Training Operator](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubeflow-distributed-training/): Run multi-node distributed PyTorch and TensorFlow training jobs using Kubeflow Training Operator with NCCL, RDMA, and shared storage.
- [Kubeflow ML Platform Setup Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubeflow-ml-platform-kubernetes/): Deploy Kubeflow as a production-ready ML platform on Kubernetes. Notebooks, pipelines, training operators, and model serving with KServe for end-to-end MLO.
- [Kubeflow Operator: Full ML Platform](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubeflow-operator-platform/): Deploy the complete Kubeflow platform on Kubernetes with the Kubeflow Operator. Covers Pipelines, Notebooks, KServe, Katib, and multi-tenant ML workflows.
- [Kubeflow Training Operator on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubeflow-training-operator/): Install Kubeflow Training Operator for distributed ML training with PyTorchJob, TFJob, and MPIJob on GPU-enabled Kubernetes clusters.
- [AI Cost Management Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubernetes-ai-cost-management/): Control AI infrastructure costs on Kubernetes with GPU utilization tracking, chargeback per team, spot instance strategies, right-sizing recommendations.
- [K8s AI Gateway: Inference Extension Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubernetes-ai-gateway-inference-extension/): Use the Kubernetes AI Gateway and Inference Extension to route LLM traffic. Model-aware routing, load balancing across inference backends.
- [AI Inference Optimization Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubernetes-ai-inference-optimization/): Optimize AI inference performance on Kubernetes. Request batching, KV cache tuning, speculative decoding, continuous batching.
- [Dynamic Resource Allocation for GPUs](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubernetes-dynamic-resource-allocation-gpu/): Use Kubernetes Dynamic Resource Allocation to schedule GPUs. DRA ResourceClaims, partitionable devices, GPU sharing, and structured parameters for accelerators.
- [GPU Node Provisioning Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubernetes-gpu-node-provisioning/): Automate GPU node provisioning for Kubernetes with Karpenter, Cluster Autoscaler, and cloud-specific node pools.
- [GPU Operator Advanced Configuration](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubernetes-gpu-operator-advanced-config/): Advanced NVIDIA GPU Operator configuration on Kubernetes. Driver containers, CUDA toolkit, GDS, GPUDirect RDMA, MIG manager, DCGM Exporter.
- [GPU Sharing with MPS and MIG on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubernetes-gpu-sharing-mps-mig/): Share NVIDIA GPUs across multiple pods using MPS time-slicing and MIG hardware partitioning. Maximize GPU utilization for inference workloads.
- [Kubeflow PyTorchJob Training K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kubernetes-kubeflow-pytorchjob/): Run distributed PyTorch training on Kubernetes with Kubeflow PyTorchJob. ElasticPolicy, nproc_per_node, RDMA configuration, and multi-GPU scaling.
- [Kueue for Batch Jobs and GPU Queues](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kueue-batch-gpu-jobs-kubernetes/): Use Kueue to manage batch job queues on Kubernetes. GPU quota, fair sharing, priority queues, ML training workloads, and multi-tenant cluster scheduling.
- [Kueue Job Queuing Fair Sharing Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/kueue-job-queuing-kubernetes/): Implement fair-share GPU job queuing with Kueue on Kubernetes. ClusterQueues, LocalQueues, ResourceFlavors, and cohort-based borrowing for multi-team AI cl.
- [LeaderWorkerSet Operator for AI Workloads](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/leaderworkerset-operator/): Deploy distributed AI training with LeaderWorkerSet Operator on Kubernetes and OpenShift for leader-worker topology with gang scheduling.
- [Llama 2 70B FP16 Model Size 140GB Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/llama-70b-model-size-gpu-requirements/): Llama 2 70B FP16 model size is 140GB. Complete GPU memory requirements for FP16, FP8, INT4 quantization, and multi-GPU tensor parallelism on Kubernetes.
- [Llama Stack on Kubernetes with NVIDIA NIM](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/llama-stack-kubernetes-nim/): Deploy Meta Llama Stack on Kubernetes for unified inference, RAG, agents, and safety APIs using NVIDIA NIM as the inference backend.
- [Autoscale LLM Inference on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/llm-autoscaling-kubernetes/): Configure Horizontal Pod Autoscaling and KEDA for LLM workloads using GPU utilization, request queue depth, and custom metrics.
- [LLM Deployment Challenges Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/llm-deployment-challenges-kubernetes/): Address common LLM deployment challenges on Kubernetes. GPU memory management, model loading optimization, inference latency tuning, batch scheduling.
- [Quantize LLMs for Efficient GPU Inference](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/llm-quantization-kubernetes/): Run quantized LLM models (GPTQ, AWQ, GGUF) on Kubernetes to reduce GPU memory requirements and serve models on smaller GPUs.
- [Kubernetes LLM Serving Frameworks Compared](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/llm-serving-frameworks-compared/): Compare vLLM, NVIDIA NIM, Triton, Ollama, and llama.cpp for serving LLMs on Kubernetes — features, performance, and when to use each.
- [ML Pipeline Automation Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/ml-pipeline-automation-kubernetes/): Automate ML pipelines on Kubernetes with Kubeflow Pipelines, Argo Workflows, and Tekton. Data preprocessing, training, evaluation, model registration.
- [MLPerf Benchmarking on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/mlperf-benchmark-kubernetes/): Run MLPerf inference and training benchmarks on Kubernetes GPU clusters to validate AI workload performance and compare hardware configurations.
- [Shared Model Caching Across Pods on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/model-caching-shared-memory/): Optimize LLM inference startup and reduce storage costs by sharing model weights across pods using emptyDir, hostPath, ReadWriteMany PVCs, and init.
- [AI Model Storage: hostPath vs PVC Inference](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/model-storage-hostpath-pvc/): Deploy AI models on Kubernetes using hostPath and PVC storage. Compare performance, security trade-offs, and production patterns for model serving.
- [ModelMesh Multi-Model Serving Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/modelmesh-multi-model-serving-kubernetes/): Deploy hundreds of ML models on shared GPU infrastructure with ModelMesh. Intelligent model loading and unloading, memory management, routing.
- [MPI Operator for Distributed Training](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/mpi-operator-kubernetes/): Deploy MPI Operator on Kubernetes for distributed GPU training with Horovod and NCCL. Run multi-node MPI jobs natively in Kubernetes pods.
- [Multi-Cloud AI Workloads Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/multi-cloud-ai-kubernetes/): Run AI workloads across multiple cloud providers with Kubernetes. GPU instance availability, spot pricing arbitrage, model portability.
- [Multi-GPU and Tensor Parallel LLM Inference](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/multi-gpu-llm-inference/): Deploy large language models across multiple GPUs using tensor parallelism with vLLM and NVIDIA NIM on Kubernetes for high-throughput inference serving.
- [Run NCCL AllGather Benchmarks Model Paralle...](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nccl-allgather-benchmark-profile/): Use all-gather NCCL tests to evaluate GPU communication behavior and throughput for tensor-parallel and model-parallel distributed AI workloads on Kubernetes.
- [Benchmark NCCL AllReduce Performance](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nccl-allreduce-benchmark-profile/): Measure NCCL AllReduce bandwidth and latency on Kubernetes to validate distributed training network performance across multi-GPU clusters.
- [NCCL_IB_DISABLE Env Variables Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nccl-environment-variables-guide/): NCCL_IB_DISABLE and NCCL_SOCKET_IFNAME environment variables guide. Complete NCCL env var reference for GPU communication tuning on Kubernetes.
- [NCCL_IB_DISABLE: When to Disable InfiniBand](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nccl-ib-disable-environment-variable/): NCCL_IB_DISABLE environment variable explained. When to set NCCL_IB_DISABLE=1 for Ethernet clusters, troubleshoot IB errors.
- [NCCL SR-IOV GDS PyTorch Configuration](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nccl-sriov-gds-pytorch-kubernetes/): Configure NCCL with SR-IOV RDMA and GPUDirect Storage on Kubernetes. PyTorch 25.11 container with NCCL 2.28, CUDA 13, MOFED 5.4, GDRCopy 2.
- [NCCL Test Benchmark Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nccl-test-benchmark-kubernetes/): Run NCCL tests on Kubernetes for GPU communication benchmarking. all_reduce_perf, all_gather_perf, multi-node bandwidth, and latency validation.
- [NCCL Topology Dump File for GPU Debugging](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nccl-topo-dump-file-gpu-topology/): Use NCCL_TOPO_DUMP_FILE to capture and analyze GPU interconnect topology in Kubernetes. Debug NVLink, NVSwitch, PCIe.
- [NIM Model Profiles and Selection on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nim-model-profiles-selection-kubernetes/): Configure NIM_MODEL_PROFILE for NVIDIA NIM deployments on Kubernetes. List profiles, select by ID or name, tune VRAM, and override with vLLM CLI args.
- [NIM Multi-Node Deployment with Helm on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nim-multinode-deployment-helm-kubernetes/): Deploy NVIDIA NIM across multiple Kubernetes nodes using Helm, LeaderWorkerSet, Ray, and vLLM. Run Llama 405B and DeepSeek-R1 on 16+ GPUs.
- [NIM LLM Support Matrix and GPU Compatibility](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nim-support-matrix-gpu-compatibility/): Complete NVIDIA NIM support matrix for Kubernetes. Supported models, profiles, precision formats, GPU compatibility, and hardware requirements per model.
- [Node Feature Discovery Operator for Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/node-feature-discovery-operator/): Install and configure Node Feature Discovery (NFD) Operator to auto-detect hardware features like GPUs, NICs, CPU flags, and USB devices on Kubernetes nodes.
- [Deploy NVIDIA Clara on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nvidia-clara-medical-ai-kubernetes/): Deploy NVIDIA Clara medical AI and drug discovery platform on Kubernetes. Run digital biology and medtech inference workloads with GPU acceleration.
- [NVIDIA Dynamo Distributed Inference](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nvidia-dynamo-distributed-inference-kubernetes/): Deploy NVIDIA Dynamo on Kubernetes for disaggregated LLM inference. KV-aware routing, prefill/decode splitting, Grove operator, and zero-config deployment.
- [Install NVIDIA GPU Operator on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nvidia-gpu-operator-install/): Deploy the NVIDIA GPU Operator to automate GPU driver, container toolkit, and device plugin management across your Kubernetes cluster.
- [NVIDIA GPU Operator Setup on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nvidia-gpu-operator-setup/): Install and configure NVIDIA GPU Operator on Kubernetes. Driver containers, toolkit, device plugin, DCGM monitoring, and ClusterPolicy setup.
- [GPU Time-Slicing vs MIG Comparison](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nvidia-gpu-time-slicing-mig/): Compare NVIDIA GPU time-slicing and MIG for K8s workloads. When to use each, performance trade-offs, and configuration examples.
- [NVIDIA H200 GPU Workloads on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nvidia-h200-gpu-kubernetes/): Deploy and optimize AI workloads on NVIDIA H200 GPUs with 141GB HBM3e memory for large model inference and training on Kubernetes.
- [NVIDIA H300 GPU Workloads on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nvidia-h300-gpu-kubernetes/): Prepare for NVIDIA H300 Blackwell-Next GPUs on Kubernetes with next-gen HBM3e memory, NVLink 5.0, and FP4 inference capabilities.
- [NVIDIA NeMo Training on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nvidia-nemo-training-kubernetes/): Deploy NVIDIA NeMo framework on Kubernetes for large language model pre-training, fine-tuning, and RLHF with multi-node GPU clusters.
- [NVIDIA Open GPU + GPUDirect RDMA + DOCA-OFE...](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nvidia-open-gpu-dma-buf-gpudirect-rdma-doca-sriov/): Deploy NVIDIA AI networking on Kubernetes: Open GPU driver with DMA-BUF, GPUDirect RDMA, DOCA-OFED, and SR-IOV VF isolation.
- [NVIDIA PeerMem GPUDirect RDMA K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nvidia-peermem-gpudirect-rdma-k8s/): Configure nvidia_peermem and ib_register_peer_memory_client for GPUDirect RDMA on Kubernetes. Module loading and modprobe invalid argument fix.
- [NVIDIA PyTorch Container K8s Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nvidia-pytorch-container-kubernetes/): Deploy nvcr.io/nvidia/pytorch containers on Kubernetes. Version selection, CUDA compatibility, multi-GPU training, and NCCL configuration for production.
- [NVIDIA Pyxis and Enroot for SLURM](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/nvidia-pyxis-slurm-containers/): Use NVIDIA Pyxis and Enroot to run GPU containers in SLURM jobs. Bridge SLURM HPC scheduling with container-native AI workloads and NGC images.
- [Physical AI and Robotics Orchestration](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/physical-ai-robotics-kubernetes/): Orchestrate physical AI and robotics fleets with Kubernetes. ROS 2 on K8s, robot fleet management, edge-cloud hybrid, NVIDIA Isaac.
- [Quantum Computing on K8s: Hybrid Workflows](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/quantum-computing-kubernetes-integration/): Run quantum computing workloads on Kubernetes. Qiskit, Cirq, PennyLane hybrid classical-quantum pipelines, quantum job scheduling, and QPU integration patterns.
- [Build a RAG Pipeline on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/rag-pipeline-kubernetes/): Deploy a Retrieval-Augmented Generation pipeline on Kubernetes using a vector database, embedding model, and LLM inference server.
- [Rebuild NIM with Custom Model on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/rebuild-nim-custom-model-kubernetes/): Step-by-step guide to deploy custom, fine-tuned, or self-hosted models with NVIDIA NIM on Kubernetes. Model-free NIM from HuggingFace, S3, NGC, or local path.
- [RetinaNet GPU Training on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/retinanet-gpu-training-kubernetes/): Train RetinaNet object detection models on Kubernetes with unlimited memlock for RDMA, CRI-O ulimit configuration, and multi-GPU distributed training.
- [Run NCCL Tests for GPU Network Validation](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/run-nccl-tests-kubernetes/): Benchmark GPU-to-GPU communication using NVIDIA nccl-tests on Kubernetes or OpenShift to validate bandwidth and latency.
- [Run:ai Distributed vLLM with NCCL](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/runai-distributed-vllm-nccl-rdma/): Deploy distributed vLLM inference on Run:ai with NCCL over NVLink and RDMA. Tensor parallelism across GPUs with NCCL debug logging, SR-IOV networking.
- [Run:ai + Dynamo Multi-Node Scheduling on K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/runai-dynamo-multinode-scheduling-kubernetes/): Deploy NVIDIA Dynamo with Run:ai v2.23 for gang scheduling and topology-aware placement. Atomic pod launches, zone co-location, and disaggregated inference.
- [Run:AI GPU Quotas on OpenShift](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/runai-gpu-quotas-openshift/): Configure Run:AI scheduler quotas for fair GPU sharing with guaranteed, over-quota borrowing, and per-tenant GPU allocation policies.
- [Run:ai NIM Distributed Inference Tutorial](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/runai-nim-distributed-inference-tutorial/): Step-by-step guide to deploy DeepSeek-R1 distributed inference on Run:ai with LeaderWorkerSet, SGLang, PVC caching, and OpenShift security.
- [Run:ai Topology-Aware Scheduling Deep Dive](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/runai-topology-aware-scheduling-kubernetes/): Configure Run:ai topology-aware scheduling for distributed AI workloads. Multi-level hierarchies, required vs preferred placement, LeaderWorkerSet.
- [Run:ai Distrib. vLLM Inference Multimodal LLMs](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/runai-vllm-distributed-inference-multimodal/): Deploy multimodal LLMs with Run:ai distributed inference and vLLM on Kubernetes. Tensor parallelism, NCCL over NVLink, GPUDirect RDMA.
- [SLURM and Kubernetes Integration](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/slurm-kubernetes-integration/): Integrate SLURM HPC workload manager with Kubernetes for hybrid AI and scientific computing. Bridge HPC batch scheduling with container orchestration.
- [TensorRT-LLM Kubernetes Deployment](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/tensorrt-llm-kubernetes-guide/): Deploy TensorRT-LLM on K8s for optimized inference. Engine building, model conversion, and serving with Triton Inference Server.
- [TensorRT-LLM vs vLLM Benchmark 2026](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/tensorrt-llm-vs-vllm-benchmark/): Compare TensorRT-LLM vs vLLM for LLM inference on Kubernetes. TTFT, throughput, GPU utilization benchmarks, and when to use each inference engine.
- [Test LLM Inference Endpoints with curl](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/test-llm-inference-endpoints/): Validate Kubernetes-hosted LLM inference services using curl against OpenAI-compatible /v1/models, /v1/completions, and /v1/chat/completions endpoints.
- [Time-Slicing vs MIG vs Full GPU Allocation](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/timeslicing-mig-full-gpu/): Compare GPU sharing strategies: time-slicing for notebooks, MIG for isolated inference, and full GPU for training workloads.
- [Triton Autoscaling with GPU Metrics](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/triton-autoscaling-gpu-metrics/): Autoscale Triton Inference Server on Kubernetes using GPU utilization, request queue depth, and inference latency metrics with KEDA and HPA.
- [Triton Inference Server vs vLLM: Which to C...](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/triton-inference-server-vs-vllm-comparison/): Compare NVIDIA Triton Inference Server vs vLLM for LLM serving on Kubernetes. Performance, multi-model support, batching, GPU utilization.
- [Triton Multi-Model Serving on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/triton-multi-model-serving/): Serve multiple LLMs simultaneously on Triton Inference Server using TensorRT-LLM and vLLM backends with model routing and GPU scheduling.
- [Triton TensorRT-LLM on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/triton-tensorrt-llm-kubernetes/): Deploy NVIDIA Triton Inference Server with TensorRT-LLM backend on Kubernetes for optimized large language model serving with GPU acceleration.
- [TensorRT-LLM vs vLLM on Triton](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/triton-tensorrt-vllm-comparison/): Compare TensorRT-LLM and vLLM backends on Triton Inference Server. When to use each, performance benchmarks, and migration strategies.
- [Triton with vLLM Backend on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/triton-vllm-kubernetes/): Deploy NVIDIA Triton Inference Server with vLLM backend on Kubernetes for flexible LLM serving with PagedAttention and continuous batching.
- [Deploying Vector Databases on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/vector-database-kubernetes/): Deploy and operate vector databases (Milvus, Weaviate, Qdrant) on Kubernetes for RAG pipelines, semantic search, and AI applications with persistent.
- [Verify NCCL RDMA Traffic with Debug Logging](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/verify-nccl-rdma-traffic-debug/): Prove NCCL uses RDMA for GPU communication on Kubernetes. Use NCCL_DEBUG and NCCL_DEBUG_SUBSYS=ALL to verify InfiniBand, RoCE.
- [vLLM Alternatives LLM Inference K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/vllm-alternatives-llm-inference-k8s/): Compare vLLM alternatives for LLM inference on Kubernetes. TensorRT-LLM, SGLang, NVIDIA NIM, Ollama, and text-generation-inference feature comparison.
- [vLLM on Huawei Ascend NPU: K8s Deployment](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/vllm-ascend-npu-inference-kubernetes/): Deploy vLLM inference on Huawei Ascend NPUs in Kubernetes. Atlas 300I/910B device plugin, vllm-ascend container image, tensor parallelism, and model serving.
- [vLLM Deployment Kubernetes Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/vllm-kubernetes-deployment-guide/): Deploy vLLM inference engine on K8s. Model loading, tensor parallelism, continuous batching, and OpenAI-compatible API setup.
- [Deploy vLLM OpenAI Container on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/vllm-openai-container-kubernetes/): Deploy the vLLM OpenAI-compatible server container on Kubernetes. Pull ghcr.io/vllm-project/vllm-openai, configure GPU resources, model loading.
- [Volcano Job minAvailable Gang Schedule](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/ai/volcano-batch-scheduling-kubernetes/): Volcano batch scheduling with minAvailable gang scheduling on Kubernetes. Job configuration, queue policies, and AI training workload scheduling.

## Autoscaling (37 recipes)

- [Kubernetes Cluster Autoscaler Setup Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/cluster-autoscaler-setup/): Configure the Cluster Autoscaler to automatically add and remove nodes based on pod scheduling demands. Covers AWS, GKE, Azure, and bare-metal setups.
- [How to Configure Cluster Autoscaler](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/cluster-autoscaler/): Automatically scale your Kubernetes cluster nodes based on workload demand. Learn to configure Cluster Autoscaler for AWS, GCP, and Azure.
- [HPA in K8s: Horizontal Pod Autoscaler Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/horizontal-pod-autoscaler/): HPA in Kubernetes explained with YAML examples. Configure Horizontal Pod Autoscaler for CPU, memory, and custom metrics with scaling policies and VPA compa.
- [How to Scale Based on Custom Metrics](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/hpa-custom-metrics/): Configure Horizontal Pod Autoscaler with custom and external metrics. Learn to scale on application-specific metrics like queue depth and request latency.
- [Karpenter Node Autoscaling for K8s on AWS](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/karpenter-node-autoscaling-kubernetes/): Deploy Karpenter for fast, flexible node autoscaling on AWS EKS. Configure NodePools, EC2NodeClasses, consolidation.
- [Karpenter Node Autoscaling for Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/karpenter-node-autoscaling/): Replace Cluster Autoscaler with Karpenter for faster, smarter node provisioning. Right-sized instances, spot fallback, consolidation, and GPU-aware scaling.
- [KEDA: Event-Driven Autoscaling for Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/keda-event-driven-autoscaling/): Scale Kubernetes workloads with KEDA based on external events: queue depth, cron schedules, Prometheus metrics, HTTP traffic, and 60+ event sources.
- [KEDA vs HPA: Event-Driven Autoscaling Expla...](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/keda-vs-hpa-autoscaling-comparison/): Compare KEDA and HPA for Kubernetes autoscaling. Scale on Kafka lag, Prometheus metrics, queue depth, cron, and custom events. KEDA vs HPA decision guide.
- [Advanced Cluster Autoscaler Config & Tuning](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/kubernetes-cluster-autoscaler-advanced/): Fine-tune the Kubernetes Cluster Autoscaler with expanders, priority-based scaling, mixed instance policies, and GPU node pool autoscaling for production c.
- [Kubernetes Cluster Autoscaler Configuration](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/kubernetes-cluster-autoscaler-configuration/): Configure Kubernetes Cluster Autoscaler: scale-down delay, node group settings, priority expander, GPU scaling, and cloud provider integration for EKS, GKE.
- [Cluster Autoscaler Kubernetes Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/kubernetes-cluster-autoscaler-guide/): Configure Kubernetes Cluster Autoscaler for automatic node scaling. Scale-down delay, expanders, priority, and integration with cloud providers.
- [Custom Metrics Autoscaling in Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/kubernetes-custom-metrics-autoscaling/): Scale Kubernetes pods on custom application metrics with Prometheus Adapter. Configure HPA with custom and external metrics beyond CPU and memory.
- [Custom Metrics with Prometheus Adapter](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/kubernetes-custom-metrics-prometheus/): Expose application metrics to Kubernetes HPA via Prometheus Adapter. Configure custom.metrics.k8s.io for HTTP requests per second, queue depth.
- [K8s Capacity Planning for Enterprise Clusters](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/kubernetes-enterprise-capacity-planning/): Right-size enterprise clusters with data-driven capacity planning. Forecast resource needs, optimize bin-packing, and plan for growth with metrics.
- [Goldilocks: VPA Recommendations Dashboard](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/kubernetes-goldilocks-vpa-dashboard/): Deploy Goldilocks to visualize VPA recommendations across namespaces. Right-size Kubernetes resource requests and limits with a web dashboard.
- [HPAv2 Multiple Metrics Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/kubernetes-horizontal-pod-autoscaler-v2/): Configure HorizontalPodAutoscaler v2 with CPU, memory, custom, and external metrics. Control scaling behavior with stabilization windows.
- [HPA Behavior and Scaling Policies](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/kubernetes-hpa-behavior-scaling-policies/): Configure HPA scaling behavior with stabilization windows, scaling policies, and rate limiting. Fine-tune scale-up and scale-down speed.
- [HPA Container Resource Metrics](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/kubernetes-hpa-container-resource-metrics/): Configure HPA to scale based on individual container metrics instead of pod-level averages. Target specific containers in multi-container pods.
- [HPA CPU Memory Autoscaling Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/kubernetes-hpa-cpu-memory-guide/): Configure HPA for CPU and memory-based autoscaling on Kubernetes. Target utilization, scaling behavior, stabilization windows, and combined metrics.
- [HPA Custom Metrics Scaling Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/kubernetes-hpa-custom-metrics-guide/): Scale Kubernetes workloads on custom Prometheus metrics with HPA. Prometheus Adapter, external metrics, and request-rate-based scaling for web services.
- [Kubernetes HPA Custom Metrics with Prometheus](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/kubernetes-hpa-custom-metrics-prometheus/): Configure Kubernetes HPA with custom Prometheus metrics. Prometheus Adapter setup, custom and external metrics, scaling on request latency, queue depth.
- [HPA Max Replicas Configuration K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/kubernetes-hpa-max-replicas-guide/): Set max replicas for Kubernetes HPA to control autoscaling ceiling. maxReplicas tuning, scaling behavior, stabilization window, and cost protection strategies.
- [HPA Prometheus Custom Metrics K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/kubernetes-hpa-prometheus-adapter/): Configure HPA with custom Prometheus metrics using prometheus-adapter on Kubernetes. Custom and external metrics, query mapping, and scaling on business KPIs.
- [HPA Tutorial Kubernetes Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/kubernetes-hpa-tutorial-guide/): Step-by-step HPA tutorial for Kubernetes. Create, monitor, and tune Horizontal Pod Autoscalers with kubectl commands and YAML examples.
- [Karpenter Node Autoscaler K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/kubernetes-karpenter-node-autoscaling/): Scale Kubernetes nodes with Karpenter. NodePool configuration, instance selection, consolidation, and cost optimization vs Cluster Autoscaler.
- [KEDA Event-Driven Autoscaling](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/kubernetes-keda-event-driven-autoscaling/): Scale Kubernetes workloads based on external events with KEDA. Kafka queue length, Prometheus metrics, cron schedules.
- [KEDA Scalers Guide Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/kubernetes-keda-scalers-guide/): Configure KEDA scalers for event-driven autoscaling on K8s. Kafka, RabbitMQ, Prometheus, and cron trigger patterns.
- [Multidimensional Pod Autoscaler (MPA)](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/kubernetes-multidimensional-pod-autoscaler/): Configure Google's Multidimensional Pod Autoscaler to scale both horizontally and vertically simultaneously. Combines HPA and VPA logic in one controller.
- [K8s Resource Optimization Strategies](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/kubernetes-resource-optimization/): Kubernetes resource optimization strategies and best practices. Right-size pods with VPA, Goldilocks dashboards, and resource allocation techniques.
- [Vertical Pod Autoscaler Deep Dive](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/kubernetes-vertical-pod-autoscaler-guide/): Configure VPA for automatic memory and CPU right-sizing in Kubernetes. Recommendation modes, update policies, VPA with HPA coexistence, and GPU workload tuning.
- [VPA Recommender Setup Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/kubernetes-vpa-recommender-guide/): Configure the VPA Recommender for Kubernetes resource right-sizing. Off mode recommendations, memory-only mode, and interpreting VPA suggestions.
- [VPA Resource Right-Sizing Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/kubernetes-vpa-resource-rightsizing/): Use Vertical Pod Autoscaler to right-size Kubernetes resource requests and limits. Off mode for recommendations, Auto mode for live adjustment.
- [OpenClaw Auto-Scaling with KEDA](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/openclaw-autoscaling-keda/): Scale OpenClaw agents based on message queue depth using KEDA event-driven autoscaling for Discord, Telegram, and Slack.
- [OpenClaw Resource Limits and Tuning](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/openclaw-resource-limits-tuning/): Size CPU, memory, and storage for OpenClaw on Kubernetes. Tuning profiles for light usage, browser automation, and production deployments.
- [VPA Setup hack/vpa-up.sh Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/vertical-pod-autoscaler-setup/): Install Vertical Pod Autoscaler with hack/vpa-up.sh on Kubernetes. Recommender, Updater, Admission Controller components and production configuration.
- [Vertical Pod Autoscaler VPA Setup Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/vertical-pod-autoscaler/): Install and configure Kubernetes Vertical Pod Autoscaler. VPA updateMode Off, Initial, Auto, recommendations, hack/vpa-up.sh install, and HPA coexistence.
- [Virtual Kubelet for Serverless K8s Scaling](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/autoscaling/virtual-kubelet-serverless-scaling/): Deploy Virtual Kubelet to burst Kubernetes workloads to serverless backends like Azure ACI, AWS Fargate, and Hashicorp Nomad for infinite scaling.

## Configuration (168 recipes)

- [Manage K8s API Versions and Deprecations](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/api-versions-deprecations/): Handle Kubernetes API version changes and deprecations. Migrate resources to stable APIs and ensure cluster upgrade compatibility.
- [ClusterPolicy MOFED Upgrade Strategy](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/clusterpolicy-mofed-upgrade/): Configure safe MOFED driver upgrade policies in the NVIDIA GPU Operator ClusterPolicy with rolling updates, node draining, and rollback procedures.
- [Fix ConfigMap Changes Not Applied to Pods](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/configmap-hot-reload-troubleshooting/): Debug ConfigMap updates not reflected in running pods. Covers volume mount propagation delays, env var immutability, and sidecar-based reload strategies.
- [Manage ConfigMaps and Secrets Effectively](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/configmap-secrets-management/): Master Kubernetes ConfigMaps and Secrets for application configuration. Learn creation methods, mounting strategies, and security best practices.
- [Configure ClusterPolicy kernelModuleType GP...](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/configure-clusterpolicy-kernel-module-type/): Understand and configure the driver.kernelModuleType field in the NVIDIA GPU Operator ClusterPolicy to choose between auto, open, and proprietary kernel.
- [Crossplane for Cloud Infrastructure Management](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/crossplane-infrastructure-management/): Use Crossplane to provision and manage cloud infrastructure resources like databases, storage, and networking using Kubernetes-native APIs and GitOps.
- [crun vs runc Container Runtime 2026](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/crun-vs-runc-container-runtime/): Compare crun vs runc container runtimes for Kubernetes. Performance benchmarks, memory usage, cgroup v2 support, and migration from runc to crun guide.
- [Create Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/custom-resource-definitions/): Extend Kubernetes API with Custom Resource Definitions. Define custom objects, configure validation schemas, and manage CRD lifecycle.
- [Databases on K8s: Memory Overcommit](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/databases-kubernetes-memory-overcommit/): Why vm.overcommit_memory must be disabled for production databases on Kubernetes. Configure guaranteed QoS, disable swap.
- [NVIDIA DOCA Driver Container in Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/doca-driver-container-kubernetes/): Deploy and configure NVIDIA DOCA Driver containers via NicClusterPolicy for RDMA, NFS-RDMA, and precompiled driver builds.
- [DOCA Driver on OpenShift with DTK](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/doca-driver-openshift-dtk/): Build and deploy precompiled NVIDIA DOCA Driver containers on OpenShift using DriverToolKit, MachineConfig, and upgrade lifecycle.
- [How to Use Downward API for Pod Metadata](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/downward-api-metadata/): Expose pod and container metadata to applications using the Downward API. Access labels, annotations, resource limits, and node information from within.
- [Multi-Node NVLink with ComputeDomains](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/dra-computedomains-nvlink/): Configure ComputeDomains for robust and secure Multi-Node NVLink (MNNVL) workloads on NVIDIA GB200 and similar systems using DRA
- [Dynamic Resource Allocation GPUs NVIDIA DRA...](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/dra-gpu-allocation/): Learn to use Kubernetes Dynamic Resource Allocation (DRA) for flexible GPU allocation, sharing, and configuration with the NVIDIA DRA Driver
- [MIG GPU Partitioning with DRA](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/dra-mig-partitioning/): Dynamically partition NVIDIA A100 and H100 GPUs using Multi-Instance GPU (MIG) technology with Dynamic Resource Allocation for flexible workload isolation
- [Mixed Accelerator Workloads with DRA](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/dra-mixed-accelerators/): Orchestrate heterogeneous accelerator workloads combining GPUs, TPUs, FPGAs, and custom AI chips using Dynamic Resource Allocation
- [TPU Allocation Dynamic Resource Allocation](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/dra-tpu-allocation/): Configure Google Cloud TPUs in Kubernetes using DRA for flexible allocation, multi-slice workloads, and optimized machine learning training
- [Configure Environment Variables and ConfigMaps](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/environment-variables-configmaps/): Manage application configuration with environment variables and ConfigMaps. Learn injection methods, mounting as files, and dynamic configuration updates.
- [GPU Operator ClusterPolicy Complete Reference](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/gpu-operator-clusterpolicy-reference/): Complete reference for the NVIDIA GPU Operator ClusterPolicy CRD covering driver, toolkit, device plugin, MOFED, GDS, MIG, and DCGM configuration options.
- [NVIDIA GPU Operator MOFED Driver Configuration](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/gpu-operator-mofed-driver/): Configure the NVIDIA GPU Operator to deploy Mellanox OFED drivers for high-performance RDMA networking on Kubernetes GPU nodes with InfiniBand and RoCE support.
- [GPU Cluster Upgrade Version Matrix](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/gpu-upgrade-version-matrix/): Maintain a version compatibility matrix for GPU Operator, Network Operator, drivers, firmware, CUDA, and OpenShift for safe upgrades.
- [How to Configure Image Pull Secrets](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/image-pull-secrets/): Pull container images from private registries using image pull secrets. Configure authentication for Docker Hub, GCR, ECR, ACR, and private registries.
- [Install Kubernetes on Fedora with kubeadm](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/install-kubernetes-fedora-kubeadm/): Step-by-step guide to install Kubernetes on Fedora Linux using kubeadm. Disable swap, configure containerd, install kubeadm kubelet kubectl.
- [IOMMU on K8s: GPU Passthrough and SR-IOV](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/iommu-gpu-passthrough-sriov-kubernetes/): Enable and configure IOMMU for GPU passthrough, SR-IOV, and VFIO on Kubernetes. Kernel parameters, IOMMU groups, device isolation, and troubleshooting guide.
- [ITMS External-to-External Registry Mirroring](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/itms-external-registry-mapping/): Configure OpenShift ImageTagMirrorSet to map external registries to your private registry. Mirror Docker Hub, GHCR, Quay.io, and NVIDIA NGC.
- [How ITMS Updates registries.conf via Machin...](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/itms-registries-conf-machineconfig/): How ITMS and IDMS update /etc/containers/registries.conf on immutable CoreOS nodes via MCO and MachineConfig. Full chain deep-dive.
- [KubeCon EU 2026 Book Giveaway Recap](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubecon-eu-2026-book-giveaway-success/): Recap of the Kubernetes Recipes book giveaway at KubeCon EU 2026 Amsterdam. Photos from the signing sessions, community highlights, and how to get your copy.
- [KubeCon EU 2026 Book Signing Events](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubecon-eu-2026-book-signing/): Join Luca Berton at two KubeCon Amsterdam events: Signal Overflow at Booking.com HQ (Mon 23 Mar) and book signing at vCluster booth #521 (Tue 24 Mar).
- [How to Manage Kubernetes Contexts and Clusters](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubeconfig-contexts/): Switch between multiple clusters efficiently. Configure kubeconfig, manage contexts, and set up secure multi-cluster access.
- [kubectl Cheat Sheet: Essential Commands](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubectl-cheat-sheet/): Complete kubectl cheat sheet with essential commands for pods, deployments, services, logs, debugging, and cluster management. Copy-paste ready examples.
- [kubectl Config: Manage Contexts and Clusters](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubectl-config-context-management/): Manage kubectl contexts with kubectl config commands. Switch clusters, delete contexts, rename, merge kubeconfigs.
- [kubectl create secret docker-registry](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubectl-create-secret-docker-registry/): kubectl create secret docker-registry with --docker-password-stdin for secure private registry auth. Complete syntax, examples, and service account setup.
- [Kubernetes 1.35 and 1.36 Upgrade Checklist](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-1-35-1-36-upgrade-checklist/): Kubernetes 1.35 and 1.36 upgrade checklist with deprecated APIs, removed features, new GA capabilities, and step-by-step migration guide for production clu.
- [K8s Admission Controllers List Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-admission-controller-list/): Complete list of Kubernetes admission controllers. Enable and disable controllers, PodSecurity, ResourceQuota, and custom validating webhooks guide.
- [Kubernetes Affinity and Anti-Affinity Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-affinity-anti-affinity/): Production guide for kubernetes affinity and anti-affinity guide. Step-by-step YAML examples, common issues, and best practices for K8s clusters.
- [Kubernetes Node and Pod Affinity Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-affinity-guide/): Configure node affinity, pod affinity, and anti-affinity rules for advanced Kubernetes scheduling. Control pod placement across zones, nodes, and topologies.
- [Kubernetes Annotations Complete Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-annotations-guide/): Use Kubernetes annotations for metadata, automation, and controller config. Common patterns for ingress annotations, Helm labels, and triggers.
- [Kubernetes API Aggregation Layer](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-api-aggregation/): Extend the Kubernetes API with custom API servers using the aggregation layer to add new resource types and functionality without modifying core components
- [Kubernetes API Deprecation Migration Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-api-deprecation-migration/): Migrate deprecated Kubernetes APIs before cluster upgrades. Detect deprecated resources with pluto, kubent, and kubectl convert.
- [API Priority and Fairness K8s Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-api-priority-fairness/): Configure Kubernetes API Priority and Fairness for API server protection. FlowSchemas, PriorityLevelConfigurations.
- [K8s API Versions Explained](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-api-versions-explained/): Understand K8s API versions: alpha, beta, stable. API deprecation policy, migration strategy, and kubectl api-versions usage.
- [Kubernetes Backup and Restore with Velero](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-backup-restore/): Backup and restore Kubernetes clusters with Velero. Covers namespace backups, scheduled backups, disaster recovery, and migration between clusters.
- [K8s Change Mgmt for Enterprise Operations](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-change-management-enterprise/): Implement ITIL-aligned change management for Kubernetes with approval gates, maintenance windows, rollback procedures, and change audit trails.
- [Kubernetes Cluster Upgrade Step-by-Step](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-cluster-upgrade-guide/): Upgrade Kubernetes clusters safely with kubeadm. Covers pre-flight checks, control plane upgrade, worker node drain, and rollback procedures.
- [How to Upgrade Kubernetes Clusters Safely](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-cluster-upgrade/): Perform Kubernetes cluster upgrades with zero downtime. Learn upgrade strategies, pre-flight checks, rollback procedures, and best practices for.
- [Essential Kubernetes Commands Reference](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-commands/): Production guide for essential kubernetes commands reference. Step-by-step YAML examples, common issues, and best practices for K8s clusters.
- [ConfigMap Best Practices K8s Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-configmap-best-practices/): ConfigMap best practices for Kubernetes applications. Size limits, binary data, environment variables vs volume mounts, and hot-reload patterns.
- [Create ConfigMap from File in Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-configmap-from-file/): Create Kubernetes ConfigMaps from files, directories, and env files with kubectl. Mount as volumes or inject as environment variables in pods.
- [Kubernetes ConfigMap Complete Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-configmap-guide/): Create and use ConfigMaps in Kubernetes for application configuration. Mount as files, inject as environment variables, and hot-reload without restarting pods.
- [K8s ConfigMap Hot Reload Without Restart](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-configmap-hot-reload/): Reload Kubernetes ConfigMaps without pod restarts. Volume-mounted auto-update, Reloader controller, checksum annotations.
- [ConfigMap Reload Patterns Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-configmap-reload-patterns/): Implement automatic ConfigMap reload in Kubernetes using volume projection, Reloader operator, checksum annotations, and inotify sidecars.
- [Immutable ConfigMaps and Secrets](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-configmap-secret-immutable/): Use immutable ConfigMaps and Secrets for performance and safety in Kubernetes. Reduce API server load, prevent accidental changes.
- [ConfigMap subPath Update Fix K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-configmap-subpath-updates/): Handle ConfigMap subPath mount limitations in Kubernetes. Why subPath mounts don't auto-update, workarounds, and alternative patterns.
- [ConfigMap Patterns Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-configmap/): Production guide for kubernetes configmap complete guide. Step-by-step YAML examples, common issues, and best practices for K8s clusters.
- [Kubernetes startupProbe Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-container-probes-startup/): Configure startupProbe for slow-starting containers to prevent premature kills. Understand interaction with liveness and readiness probes.
- [Container Runtime Comparison K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-container-runtime-comparison/): Compare Kubernetes container runtimes: containerd vs CRI-O vs Kata Containers. Performance, security, and use cases for each runtime in production.
- [Kubernetes Cordon, Drain, and Uncordon Nodes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-cordon-drain-node/): Production guide for kubernetes cordon, drain, and uncordon nodes. Step-by-step YAML examples, common issues, and best practices for K8s clusters.
- [Cost Optimization Strategies Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-cost-optimization-strategies/): Reduce Kubernetes cloud costs with right-sizing, spot instances, cluster autoscaler tuning, resource quotas, and idle workload detection.
- [How to Optimize Kubernetes Costs](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-cost-optimization/): Reduce cloud costs in Kubernetes clusters. Right-size resources, use spot instances, implement autoscaling, and monitor spending effectively.
- [CRD Development Kubernetes Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-crd-development-guide/): Design and implement Kubernetes Custom Resource Definitions. Schema validation, status subresource, printer columns, conversion webhooks.
- [Kubernetes CronJob concurrencyPolicy Explained](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-cronjob-concurrencypolicy/): Configure Kubernetes CronJob concurrencyPolicy: Allow, Forbid, and Replace. Control overlapping job execution, prevent duplicate runs, and handle slow jobs.
- [Crossplane Infrastructure as Code](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-crossplane-infrastructure/): Manage cloud infrastructure from Kubernetes with Crossplane. Composite Resources, Compositions, provider configuration.
- [Kubernetes Disaster Recovery for Enterprise](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-disaster-recovery-enterprise/): Kubernetes disaster recovery with Velero backup and restore. Cross-region replication, etcd snapshots, multi-cluster failover, and RTO/RPO strategies.
- [Kubernetes Disaster Recovery Planning](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-disaster-recovery-plan/): Build a Kubernetes disaster recovery plan with etcd backups, Velero, cross-region replication, and RTO/RPO targets for production clusters.
- [Docker Registry Secret kubectl](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-docker-registry-secret/): Create Kubernetes docker-registry secrets with kubectl. --docker-password-stdin, .dockerconfigjson format, and automating registry authentication.
- [Downward API Kubernetes Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-downward-api-guide/): Expose pod and container metadata to applications using the Downward API. Environment variables, volume files, fieldRef, resourceFieldRef, and common patterns.
- [Kubernetes Downward API: Pod Metadata in Env](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-downward-api-pod-metadata/): Expose pod metadata to containers using Kubernetes Downward API. Access pod name, namespace, node name, labels, annotations.
- [Env Variables from ConfigMap K8s](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-env-configmap-secrets/): Inject environment variables from ConfigMaps and Secrets in Kubernetes. envFrom, valueFrom, configMapKeyRef, and secretKeyRef patterns.
- [envFrom ConfigMapRef Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-envfrom-configmapref/): Inject all ConfigMap keys as environment variables using envFrom configMapRef in Kubernetes. Bulk injection, prefix, and selective key patterns.
- [Kubernetes Environment Variables Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-environment-variables/): Set Kubernetes environment variables with envFrom, configMapRef, secretKeyRef, and the Downward API. Variable ordering, fieldRef, and best practices.
- [etcd Backup and Restore Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-etcd-backup-restore/): Back up and restore etcd for Kubernetes disaster recovery. Automated snapshots, S3 upload, point-in-time restore.
- [Kubernetes etcd Operations and Maintenance](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-etcd-operations-guide/): Manage etcd for Kubernetes: backup, restore, compaction, defragmentation, member management, and disaster recovery procedures.
- [etcd Performance Tuning Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-etcd-performance-tuning/): Tune etcd for Kubernetes cluster performance. Disk IOPS requirements, compaction, defragmentation, and monitoring etcd health metrics.
- [Kubernetes Finalizers Explained](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-finalizers-explained/): How Kubernetes finalizers work: prevent resource deletion until cleanup completes. Custom finalizer patterns, stuck resource recovery.
- [K8s Finalizers: Prevent Premature Deletion](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-finalizers-guide/): How Kubernetes finalizers work to prevent premature resource deletion. Add, remove, and troubleshoot stuck finalizers on PVCs, namespaces, and custom resources.
- [Finalizers and Ownership Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-finalizers-ownership-guide/): Understand Kubernetes finalizers and owner references for resource lifecycle management. Prevent resource leaks, implement cleanup logic.
- [How to Use Kubernetes Finalizers](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/kubernetes-finalizers/): Manage resource cleanup with Kubernetes finalizers. Implement custom cleanup logic and understand how finalizers prevent premature resource deletion.

- [Pause and Unpause MCP Rollouts](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/mcp-pause-unpause-rollout/): Temporarily pause MachineConfigPool rollouts to batch multiple MachineConfig changes or coordinate with maintenance windows. Unpause to resume node updates.

- [MOFED and DOCA Driver Building for OpenShift](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/mofed-doca-driver-building-openshift/): Build NVIDIA MOFED and DOCA drivers for OpenShift using DriverToolKit, Buildah, and MachineConfig for RDMA and GPU networking.

- [How to Configure Resource Quotas per Namespace](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/resource-quotas-namespace/): Implement resource quotas to limit CPU, memory, and object counts per namespace. Ensure fair resource allocation across teams and environments.
- [How to Configure Resource Quotas](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/resource-quotas/): Limit resource consumption per namespace with ResourceQuotas. Control CPU, memory, storage, and object counts to ensure fair cluster sharing.
- [Set Resource Requests and Limits Properly](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/resource-requests-limits/): Master Kubernetes resource management with proper CPU and memory requests and limits. Avoid OOMKills, throttling, and resource contention.
- [ResourceQuota and LimitRange for GPUs](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/resourcequota-limitrange-gpu/): Configure ResourceQuota and LimitRange for GPU workloads with per-tenant caps on GPU, CPU, memory, and object counts in Kubernetes.
- [Restore Scaled Deployments After Node Drain](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/restore-scaled-deployments-after-drain/): Restore deployments scaled down for maintenance. Verify node health, check pod scheduling, and confirm service availability.
- [RHCOS for OpenShift Nodes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/rhcos-openshift-node-management/): Understand and manage Red Hat Enterprise Linux CoreOS (RHCOS) for OpenShift nodes including MachineConfig, ignition, OS updates, and node customization.
- [Scale Deployments to Unblock Node Drains](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/scale-deploy-unblock-drain/): Safely scale down deployments that block node drains due to PDB violations. Record original replicas, scale to zero, drain, then restore after the node returns.
- [Kubernetes Scheduler Configuration and Tuning](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/scheduler-configuration-tuning/): Customize the Kubernetes scheduler with scheduling profiles, plugins, and advanced placement strategies for optimal pod placement and resource utilization
- [Switch to Open NVIDIA Kernel Modules on Ope...](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/switch-proprietary-to-open-kernel-modules/): Step-by-step guide to migrate the NVIDIA GPU Operator from proprietary to open kernel modules on OpenShift, enabling DMA-BUF and GPUDirect Storage support.
- [Tune NCCL Env Variables for RDMA & Ethernet](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/configuration/tune-nccl-env-rdma-ethernet/): Apply safe NCCL environment variable profiles for RDMA-capable and Ethernet-only GPU clusters to maximize collective communication throughput.

## Deployments (210 recipes)

- [How to Implement A/B Testing with Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/ab-testing-kubernetes/): Route traffic between application versions for A/B testing. Use service mesh, ingress, and custom routing rules to validate features with real users.
- [Air-Gapped Image Import for OpenShift Clusters](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/air-gapped-image-import-openshift/): Import container images into disconnected OpenShift clusters. Use podman save/load and internal registries when DNS and TLS block external pulls.
- [Air-Gapped OpenShift with Quay Mirror](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/airgapped-openshift-quay-mirror/): Deploy OpenShift in air-gapped environments with local Quay registry mirror, ImageDigestMirrorSet, and custom CatalogSources.
- [Argo Workflows on Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/argo-workflows-kubernetes/): Production guide for argo workflows on kubernetes. Step-by-step YAML examples, common issues, and best practices for K8s clusters.
- [ArgoCD App of Apps Pattern](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/argocd-app-of-apps-pattern/): Implement the ArgoCD App of Apps pattern to manage multiple applications from a parent Application for cluster bootstrapping.
- [ArgoCD App of Apps with Sync Waves](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/argocd-app-of-apps-sync-waves/): Combine the ArgoCD App of Apps pattern with sync waves to bootstrap entire clusters in dependency order, from CRDs and operators to application workloads.
- [ArgoCD ApplicationSets for Multi-Tenant GPUs](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/argocd-applicationsets-multi-tenant/): Use ArgoCD ApplicationSets to auto-discover and provision GPU tenant overlays from Git directories with per-tenant sync policies.

- [Detect ArgoCD Shadow Updates Out-of-Band](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/argocd-shadow-update-detection/): Detect and prevent ArgoCD shadow updates where manual kubectl changes bypass GitOps. Configure self-heal, sync, and drift detection.

- [ArgoCD Sync Waves for Database Migrations](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/argocd-sync-waves-database-migration/): Use ArgoCD sync waves and PreSync hooks to run database migrations before deploying application code, with rollback strategies.

- [K8s Local Development with Minikube and Kind](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/kubernetes-local-development/): Set up local Kubernetes clusters for development with Minikube, Kind, and k3d. Covers installation, configuration, local registries, and hot-reload workflows.

- [Multi-Container Pod Patterns](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/kubernetes-multi-container-patterns/): Implement sidecar, ambassador, and adapter patterns in Kubernetes pods. Share volumes and network namespace between containers for modular architectures.
- [K8s Multi-Cluster Management Guide](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/kubernetes-multicluster-management-guide/): Kubernetes multi-cluster management guide. Federation, Cluster API, Rancher, and GitOps patterns for fleet management across production environments.
- [Native Sidecar Containers in K8s: Complete ...](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/kubernetes-native-sidecar-containers-guide/): Use native sidecar containers in Kubernetes v1.33+. InitContainer restartPolicy Always, lifecycle ordering, logging sidecars, service mesh.
- [Node Affinity Scheduling Kubernetes](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/kubernetes-node-affinity-scheduling/): Configure node affinity rules for Kubernetes pod scheduling. Required vs preferred affinity, label selectors, and combining with taints and tolerations.
- [Node Maintenance and Drain Operations](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/kubernetes-node-maintenance-drain/): Safely drain Kubernetes nodes for maintenance with cordon, drain, and uncordon. Handle PDBs, DaemonSets, local storage.

- [Kubernetes and OpenShift Upgrade Strategy](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/kubernetes-openshift-upgrade-overview/): Complete upgrade strategy for Kubernetes and OpenShift clusters. Understand patch, minor, and major versions, upgrade paths.
- [Kubernetes Operator Pattern Explained](https://kubernetes.recipes/recipes/deployments/kubernetes-operator-pattern/): Build and use Kubernetes Operators for automated application management. Covers the operator pattern, CRDs, controller-runtime, and Operator SDK.
